title: Back pain with red flag negatives and work certificate request

transcript.speaker: GP
transcript.text: How did the back pain start?

transcript.speaker: Patient
transcript.text: I lifted boxes three days ago. Lower back pain, no pain down the legs.

transcript.speaker: GP
transcript.text: Any numbness around the saddle area, leg weakness, bladder or bowel changes, fever, cancer history, unexplained weight loss, or trauma?

transcript.speaker: Patient
transcript.text: No. I can walk. No bladder or bowel changes. No fever or cancer.

transcript.speaker: GP
transcript.text: Exam shows lumbar muscle tenderness, normal leg strength and reflexes, straight leg raise negative. This fits mechanical back pain.

transcript.speaker: Patient
transcript.text: Can I have a certificate? I work in a warehouse.

transcript.speaker: GP
transcript.text: Yes, modified duties for one week: avoid heavy lifting and prolonged bending. Stay active, use heat and paracetamol/ibuprofen if safe. Return urgently for weakness, numbness, bladder/bowel changes, saddle numbness, fever, or worsening pain.

candidate_notes.note: Acute mechanical low back pain after lifting boxes 3 days ago. No radicular pain. Red flags denied: saddle anaesthesia, weakness, bladder/bowel change, fever, cancer, weight loss, trauma. Exam: lumbar muscle tenderness, normal strength/reflexes, SLR negative. Plan: stay active, heat, paracetamol/ibuprofen if safe. Certificate for modified duties 1 week, avoid heavy lifting/prolonged bending. Urgent review red flags documented.
Low back pain after lifting. Sciatica symptoms present. Given analgesia and medical certificate. Review if not better.

S: Low back pain after lifting boxes, no leg radiation. No bladder/bowel symptoms, weakness, saddle numbness, fever, cancer history, weight loss, trauma. O: lumbar tenderness, normal strength/reflexes, SLR negative. A: mechanical back pain. P: analgesia and activity; modified duties certificate.

Acute mechanical low back pain. Red flags actively checked and denied: saddle numbness, weakness, bladder/bowel changes, fever, cancer/weight loss, trauma. Normal neuro exam. Stay active, heat, simple analgesia/NSAID if safe. Work certificate: modified duties 1 week, no heavy lifting/prolonged bending. Urgent review for red flags or worsening pain.

CDSS back-pain pathway: no cauda equina, infection, malignancy, fracture, or neurologic red flags. Exam normal neurologically; SLR negative. Mechanical back pain after lifting. Plan: remain active, heat, paracetamol/ibuprofen if safe, modified work duties for 1 week. Safety-net red flags and return thresholds documented.
